description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy installation consisting of a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels supported by a single vertical pole. The solar panel array is composed of eight individual modules arranged in a 2x4 grid, with a metallic frame holding them together. The pole is anchored into a concrete base, which is embedded in a patch of grassy terrain, providing stability and resistance to wind loads. The solar panels are tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture, and the mounting structure appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or steel. No visible electrical cabling or inverter housing is present, and the installation is designed for outdoor use in open urban or park environments.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with eight modules on a metal pole anchored in concrete, set on a grassy patch. The panels are tilted for optimal sunlight capture and supported by a sturdy metallic frame.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular solar panel array facing forward, with the supporting pole and concrete base visible on grassy terrain.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the solar panels and the slim profile of the array, with the pole and base seen from the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array, the supporting pole, and the concrete base, with the panel grid structure clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the panel tilt and the mounting structure from the other side, emphasizing the panel thickness and support.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (likely south), orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, ground-mounted with a steel or aluminum pole anchored in a concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: 2x4 grid of solar panels in a single rectangular array
